name: Macrozamia
description: [FILL IN] Macrozamia is a genus of cycads endemic to Australia, comprising the majority of Australia's native cycad diversity. Species range from small, acaulescent (near-stemless) plants to substantial arborescent forms. They inhabit a variety of environments from tropical rainforest to dry eucalypt woodland and heathland across all mainland states.
etymology: [FILL IN] From the Greek 'makros' (large) and 'Zamia', referring to the large size of many species relative to Zamia.

conservation_status.notes: [FILL IN] Several species are listed as Vulnerable or Endangered under Australian law. Many are protected under CITES Appendix II.

distribution.habitat: [FILL IN] Highly variable; eucalypt woodland, dry sclerophyll forest, heathland, tropical rainforest margins.

morphology.growth_form: [FILL IN] Varies from near-stemless (acaulescent) to arborescent forms.
morphology.stem: [FILL IN] Subterranean to erect cylindrical trunk; persistent leaf bases armour the stem.
morphology.leaves: [FILL IN] Pinnately compound; leaflets narrow to broad, sometimes twisted; new leaves often bronze-coloured.
morphology.reproductive_structures: [FILL IN] Dioecious; male cones narrow and cylindrical; female cones barrel-shaped, often brightly coloured.
morphology.seeds_or_spores: [FILL IN] Seeds with fleshy, brightly coloured (red to orange) sarcotesta.
morphology.roots: [FILL IN] Coralloid roots with nitrogen-fixing cyanobacteria.

ecology.pollination: [FILL IN] Primarily by thrips; some wind pollination.
ecology.seed_dispersal: [FILL IN] Large birds (such as Emu, parrots) and mammals.
ecology.symbioses: [FILL IN] Nitrogen-fixing cyanobacteria in coralloid roots; adaptations to fire (geophytic or fire-stimulated flushing).

uses.cultural: [FILL IN] Seeds used as food by Aboriginal Australians following careful preparation to remove toxins.
uses.food: [FILL IN] Seeds processed (soaked, dried, ground) for traditional food uses.
uses.horticultural: [FILL IN] Many species widely cultivated in Australian gardens and internationally.

cultivation.soil: [FILL IN] Sandy, well-drained soil; tolerates poor soils.
cultivation.light: [FILL IN] Full sun to partial shade.
cultivation.propagation: [FILL IN] Seed; offsets in some species.
cultivation.notes: [FILL IN] All parts highly toxic; fire-adapted species may need periodic burning to stimulate cone production.

notable_species.notes: [FILL IN] One of the largest species.
